text.title: api-gateway-graphql Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-gateway-graphql as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Accepting a supergraph without running rover supergraph compose to verify composition.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs miss @inaccessible and @override directives; federation v1 and v2 directives get mixed. Composition failures only appear at router startup, causing production outages.

failure-mode.description: Mixing Apollo Federation v1 and v2 directives in subgraph schemas.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: v1: @extends type, @external on key field; v2: extend schema @link, @key without @external on owned fields. Mixed schemas fail composition silently in some cases and loudly in others.

failure-mode.description: No query depth or complexity limits in production.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Arbitrary deeply nested queries (depth 50+) can trigger cartesian product data fetches across subgraphs, saturating the router and all subgraph services.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Exposes the full schema as an attack surface map. Disable introspection; provide schema documentation via Apollo Studio or Redoc instead.
